channel.title: Comments on: Alaskan Brewing Company – Alaskan Winter, Ep. 94
channel.description: Beer America TV is the first beer web site dedicated to the specialty craft brewing world with video on industry experts and insiders plus more!

item.title: By: Keith Barnett
item.dc:creator: Keith Barnett
item.description: John, Paul, Living up here in Wa state I am very familiar with the great beers brewed by Alaskan Brewing Co. Thank you for your evaluation of the Winter Ale. I hope they sent you some of their Witbier and of course their Alaskan Amber which is hugely popular in this part of the world. Anyway great job guys hope to see more northwest beers soon on your show. Keith. p.s.John next time you do a show don't have Judge Judy playing in the background. Don't want people to get the wrong idea about Moon River! :)
item.content:encoded:
John, Paul,
Living up here in Wa state I am very familiar with the great beers brewed by Alaskan Brewing Co. Thank you for your evaluation of the Winter Ale. I hope they sent you some of their Witbier and of course their Alaskan Amber which is hugely popular in this part of the world. Anyway great job guys hope to see more northwest beers soon on your show.
Keith.
p.s.John next time you do a show don’t have Judge Judy playing in the background. Don’t want people to get the wrong idea about Moon River! 🙂

item.title: By: Paul
item.dc:creator: Paul
item.description:
Thanks for the comment Ashley! I know we don't "rate" beers here on BATV, but all of the beers we tried from Alaskan were excellent!! I urge all of our viewers to try it if you can find it.
Paul
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the comment Ashley! I know we don’t “rate” beers here on BATV, but all of the beers we tried from Alaskan were excellent!! I urge all of our viewers to try it if you can find it.

Paul

item.title: By: Ashley
item.dc:creator: Ashley
item.description:
Hi Paul and John, Great Review! To answer your question - Alaskan Beers are currently available in 10 western states including Alaska (AK, AZ, CA, CO, ID, MT, NV, OR, WA, WY). It's nearly 1000 miles from Juneau just to Seattle, but we're slowly making our way to you in the Southwest :)
Also, the berry, almost fruity flavor you are getting is actually from the Sitka Spruce tips. We use the Sitka spruce not only because it is native to our home here in Southeast Alaska, but because it has an almost berry-like flavor with a surprisingly light pine or resinous profile. Glad we could share a little taste of AK with your viewers - looking forward to more Beer AmericaTV reviews, Cheers!
Ashley
item.content:encoded:
Hi Paul and John,
Great Review!
To answer your question – Alaskan Beers are currently available in 10 western states including Alaska (AK, AZ, CA, CO, ID, MT, NV, OR, WA, WY). It’s nearly 1000 miles from Juneau just to Seattle, but we’re slowly making our way to you in the Southwest 🙂

Also, the berry, almost fruity flavor you are getting is actually from the Sitka Spruce tips. We use the Sitka spruce not only because it is native to our home here in Southeast Alaska, but because it has an almost berry-like flavor with a surprisingly light pine or resinous profile.
Glad we could share a little taste of AK with your viewers – looking forward to more Beer AmericaTV reviews, Cheers!

Ashley
